feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Sap Support when MFA authentication is active
entry.content:
Hello expert.

In the Tinexta environment, to connect to the Datasphere environment, an MFA authentication process has been put in place. I was wondering, in such circumstances, which are the Sap best practices to enable the user devoted to you (the Sap Support User) to connect to the Datasphere environment. Now, to enable to your team to connect, we have temporally disabled the MFA authentication process, but I suppose that this cannot be a stable solution, even considering that in the future we will need your assistance for the Production environment, where we will not be authorized to disable the MFA authentication process. I hope you got my point.

Waiting for your reply

Regards

Andrea

entry.title: Delta extraction is not working in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: I have used CDS View Entity for delta extraction and also used all the annotations. But delta load is not working. Is delta load not work for CDS View Entity?

entry.title: Information Design Tool: The application was unable to start correctly 0xc000007b Click to close th
entry.content:
Installed Business Objects Client   2025 
BIPLATCLNT4400_0-80008792.EXE 

Cannot open Information Design Tool: The application was unable to start correctly 0xc000007b Click to close the Application

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Object Store activation in partner tenant
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

from Version 2025.07 the provisioning of the object store is available from "tenant configuration".

we have a partner tenant without the possibility to access to "tenant configuration" page.

How we can activate the object store for a partner tenant? ( i think it's a TDD environment) 

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.title: Leverage Input parameters from CDS in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We have S/4HANA Rise as the Source system that is connected to Datasphere. The CDS has input parameters, how to leverage the CDS with input parameters into the datasphere.

What capabilities in datasphere could be leveraged to consume these CDS views with input parameters.

@SAPSupport 

@harjisingh 

@Vera_Jiang 
@William_Yu 
@junwu 
@ANKIT_CHAUHAN 

@DonWilliams 

@ClearQueries 

@Sandra_Rossi 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Casting issue after projection.
entry.content:
We Have been getting the following error message after a projection since some time ago, and have been unable to find the cause. 

internal error: Error opening the cursor for the remote database <DWC_BWBRIDGESPACE.BWBRIDGE> [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] General error;403 internal error: An error occurred while executing the query. The server name: "DWCHDL2" internal error: [SAP][ODBC Driver][SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Relational Engine]Data exception - data type conversion is not possible. -- (dflib/dfe_Cast.cxx 888) The problematic query: "SELECT TOP 1000 "/BIC/OUZ1_APOS01"."RPA_SAT", SUBSTRING("/BIC/OUZ1_APOS01"."PLANT",0,4), SUBSTRING("/BIC/OUZ1_APOS01"."RPA_WID",0,10), SUBSTRING("/BIC/OUZ1_APOS01"."DOC_NUMBER",0,10),


More about the set-up. 
We are orking with SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge here we combine our remote tables into one view layer 2. We have a 0 layer where nothing changes as a shell around the remote table and a 1 layer where we do change some data types, however this is only the calendar date to a date type and a sales time object to a Time, no other changes are made before the error arises, and then as mentioned we create a union between different layer 1 objects into a layer 2 view, and this is where the issue starts showing up.  

We utilize this union view in a Fact view, where we only require a sub selection of all available fields, which is when the above error shows up. Until a couple of days ago this error was pushed until the reporting layer, this however is luckily no longer the case and it only shows at fact level, however the cause is still unknown. 

The data type do not seem to be different over the Cold store and Reporting or warm store data and the error does not show up in every case. The question is about any possible causes or solutions that could be looked into, because thus far we have not been able to find any, any ideas would be appreciated.

entry.title: Datasphere CDS View live modelling
entry.content:
Within our SAP Datasphere we are interested in providing live insights and reducing data footprint (nothing unusual so far). However, therefore we need to import remote tables based on CDS Views.

Unfortunately, when trying to select typical CDS Views like I_JOURNALENTRYITEM which would be a perfect fit for live data based on remote tables these views are not displayed when chosing remote tables within our system as they miss required annotation.

 

For modelling purposes based on live views it would be easiest to have CDS Views which are based on the table only and are kind of a table wrapping CDS View for further modelling in Datasphere.

With the link below it is easy to identify the views that are based on an object (e.g. a table).

Cloudification Repository Viewer

However, there is mostly no wrapping view that can be used in datasphere on the table directly.

Most likely it behaves the same for other customers as well as it should be a common use case that modelling based on live data is preferred over replication and modelling is easier when adressing a table or view that displays the tables cells.

Do you have any hints for me or is it really required that every customer creates his own CDS Views based on the table with the required annotations for live modelling?

Thank you very much in advance

entry.title: AP Datasphere - is it possible to use hierarchies with directory in Business Builder?
entry.content:
Hello,

Could anyone please let me know if it is possible to use Hierarchies with Directory in the Datasphere Business Builder? I did use them with Analytical models before and they work seamlessly!

However, I did read a few blogs from the year 2023 which state the only parent-child hierarchies are possible with the Business Builder and that is with limitations with the drill down on SAC, is it still the case?

Kind regards,

Ashwin

entry.title: Issue with OData Requests in Analytical Models Following the Latest SAP Datasphere Update
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

Recently, we discovered an issue in SAP Datasphere: analytical models that use views with associations as their data source no longer process OData requests as they used to. This problem seems to have emerged after the latest SAP Datasphere update.

Until a few weeks ago, OData requests on analytical models based on views with associations were working correctly. However, now these requests are failing. We attempted to resolve the issue by recreating both the view and the analytical model, but without success.

Has anyone else experienced this problem following the latest SAP Datasphere update? If so, have you found any workaround or solution to restore the functionality of OData requests on analytical models using views with associations?

Any insights or experiences you can share would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Datasphere Replication Flow - output file name with timestamp
entry.content:
Hello Datasphere experts,

Is it possible to add timestamp in target filename in outbound Replication Flow? If yes, how to do this?

We must send several outbound files per day and would like to have time stamp in the target file name. Any other way to do this? 

Br, Matti

entry.title: Datasphere - Graphical vs SQL Views. Procs and Cons of each?
entry.content:
This is currently a debate between two developers, one a seasoned SQL developer and the other one who prefers graphical interfaces.

We both have similar arguments - transparency and ease of interpretation; speed; robustness

However you could argue for and against either based on all of the above.

Scouring the web on the topic (but not just related to Datasphere) some of the responses are:

Three reasons you would prefer the GUI:

  • The organisation that owns the dataset wants to protect the data infrastructure from poorly written SQL
  • Someone only asks rudimentary questions on small to medium sized data sets
  • Someone does not know how to write SQL and does not want to learn

Three reasons you would prefer using SQL:

  • SQL allows for more complex queries not easily achievable through the GUI, often with functions not available in the GUI Expression editor
  • Experienced developers can write SQL queries more quickly than navigating the GUI
  • Once written, a SQL query can be amended for another purpose, without starting from scratch

There are some interesting articles out there:

https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1ELHonECz4Z0bgBle7Ld_v2gM8xZFPFKUmjYznKGkCFY/present?slide=id.p1

https://www.nextlytics.com/blog/sap-datasphere-sql-or-graphical-views-making-an-educated-decision

Graphical views may display the relationships between tables in terms of linkages, but you have to click on the joins to see the fields used. You also have to click on projections to see fields excluded, and functions to see transformations. A view with multiple tables, whilst on the surface presents a simplistic view, requires time to fully understand the ‘what’ and ‘how’ of what it is doing.

SQL views, when written applying good coding practices, present everything in one place – the sources, the joins (both the connections and the fields used), the conditions, any transformational logic and which fields are output and with what names/aliases. Including comments can also explained the 'Why' too

The argument that SQL queries can be overly complicated and difficult to interpret can be mitigated by splitting the code into sections, or even separate views. There is no necessity for monster scripts to be produced. Simpler layered views can be used for multiple purposes, GUI or SQL.

To focus purely on one as a corporate approach appears to be throwing the baby out with the bath water, when it should be horses for courses.

On a personal note I am that seasoned SQL developer, which may explain any unintentional bias herein. That said, I have a list of additional pros for SQL, one being:

  • One that recently came to light was rebuilding downstream views because a change was required to the initial view on the source table.
  • For SQL I copied the script, dropped the view, edited the high-level view, re-created the low-level view, pasted the script and made the necessary amendments. 2 minutes
  • For a graphical view, containing 5 tables, this takes much longer and the risk of missing something – a filter in a projection, a transformation in a function – is considerably higher.

I’d be interested to know your thoughts on the matter?

entry.title: Datasphere change a remote table to a view
entry.content:
• I have a space where I replicate tables from an ECC system then use these tables in the SQL data models. 
  • Now I have a need to create a historical version and current version of those tables and replicate to Datapshere using Filters. 
  • Take BSEG for instance.  I will have a h_BSEG remote table replicated with data <= 2024.  Then a c_BSEG replicating data >= 2025.  I will only have to do updates to the c_BSEG saving time in replications. 
  • I need to do this because our version of SAC does not support realtime replication and we don't have anything like SLT.
  • Then I will union the historical data h_BSEG with the current data c_BSEG in a view BSEG.  I will do this all in a different space. 
  • The problem I have is all my queries use the remote table in the original space and I will have to do a lot of work to update the queries replacing the source table reference.
  • I was wondering if I could replace my remote table with a view pointing to my union view.  I know I could do it if the remote table does not exist but I cannot delete it since there are dependencies.

Anyone know a way to replace my Remote table so it stays the same name in the same space so all my queries it is shared to in other spaces still work?

Appreciate any suggestions.

Bart

entry.title: Generic Delta Extraction using Function Module not working in Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hello experts,
tell me how to fix custom Data Source in ECC so that Replicatin Flow works in Initial and Delta mode. 

Сustom Data Source in ECC created according to instructions
Generic Delta Extraction using Function Module alo... - SAP Community

In DataSphere error:

 

Thanks)

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Connection couldn’t be established.
entry.content:
Good day,

I trust you are all well.

I am experiencing an issue when trying to connect to an Azure Synapse SQL Database via Cloud Connector.

The Azure Synapse SQL Server hostname is accessible via Cloud Connector however I get the below error when creating the Connection on SAP Datasphere:

Connection "*** Azure Synapse DB" couldn’t be established. - Data flows can’t be used because of errors in the connection. - Replication flows can’t be used because of errors in the connection. - Remote tables are disabled.
Data Flows: Cause: Communication with DS backend was unexpectedly closed
Replication Flows: Cause: Communication with DS backend was unexpectedly closed

Regards,

Brandon

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: Analytic Model - Variable - Option "Dynamic Default" doesn't fill the prompt anymore
entry.content:
Hi,

just 2-3 weeks ago, my "dynamic default"-variables were filling the prompts (with values i have expected). Now, these variables don't fill the prompt anymore.

The last two comments of the following blog post express the same issue:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/datasphere-populating-dynamic-default-values-in-analytical-model-variables/ba-p/14009854

Anyone else who got this problem?

entry.title: Exploring SAP Joule Integration Options with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm exploring the possibility of integrating SAP Joule with SAP Datasphere — either via direct service availability or custom architecture.

Specifically, I’m interested in understanding:

  • Is SAP Joule currently accessible as a standalone service on SAP BTP or only embedded within specific SAP applications?

  • Are there any supported or recommended approaches for using Joule’s capabilities in conjunction with SAP Datasphere?

  • Does the roadmap include broader availability of Joule APIs or SDKs that developers could leverage for custom integrations?

I’d appreciate any guidance, documentation links, or experience others have had exploring this kind of setup.

Thanks in advance!

Joule SAP Datasphere

entry.title: How can I transport tables, views and folders from one space to another?
entry.content:
I want to transport an analytical model from one space to another, this model have a depencency from multiple local tables and views, and each one of those are in diferent folders.

How can I transport the model and all it's dependencies to another space mantaining the folders association of each asset? 

I tried the Export CSN/JSON and import it in the other space, but that doesn't import the folders.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Error Connecting Power BI to Datasphere with HANA ODBC Driver
entry.content:
Hi, we facing an issue connecting Power BI to Datasphere with HANA ODBC Driver. We were able to establish the PBI to Datasphere ODBC connection through local desktop/machine but was not able to have a successful connection through PBI gateway.  The error that we are facing is "[SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] Communication link failure;-10709 Connection failed (RTE:[89013].Socket closed by peer". (refer to attachment for screenshot)

We went through the below notes and maintained the PBI public and local IP addresses but still not able to establish the connection:

  • 3531754 - Error Connecting Power BI to Datasphere tenant with HANA ODBC Driver
  • 3036312 - ODBC connection test to SAP Datasphere drops error Communication link failure; -10709 connection failed 

What other areas should we be checking our configurations apart from maintaining PBI's IP addresses in Datasphere's IP Allowlist. Is there a way we can trace the security logs within SAP Datasphere if there were attempts of connect from external systems?

entry.title: Error connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata - Error
entry.content:
Error connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata - Error

Good morning, team.

Thank you for your valuable help. When connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Commerce Cloud via Odata, I'm getting the following error:

Data Flows: Cause: Invalid odata connection! Getting odata metadata failed because of <HTTP/1.1 400 > Get $metadata response: <?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"><code/><message xml:lang="en">Must be an entity set instead of an entity: 'Products'.</message></error> org.apache.olingo.odata2.api.ep.EntityProviderException: Tag with name 'Edmx' was not found.
Remote Tables: Could not establish a secure connection because the server certificate couldn’t be validated. Please upload the certificate of the server or the certificate of the signing authority (CA). If you’re not authorized, please contact your administrator.

Do you know what this refers to?

I would greatly appreciate your help.

entry.title: Granting End User Access to a Specific Analytical Model in SAP DataSphere
entry.content:
Hi All

i have a requirement in SAP DataSphere,  I would like to grant access to a particular Analytical Model from a specific Space to a specific user, but not provide access to other models or objects in the same space.

As far as I know, Data Access Control does not support object-level access restriction. Also, creating a separate space for each model is not a feasible option for us, as we have many such scenarios

Appreciate any guidance or best practices you can share
